topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my screen, a jarring intrusion into a perfectly ordinary Tuesday afternoon. My breath hitched. The name staring back at me was etched in a familiar, unwelcome script: Mark. Mark, the architect of my third-grade misery, the one whose shadow I’d spent the better part of a decade trying to outrun. A friend request. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly. I reread the profile picture—a poorly filtered selfie from a summer camp years ago—and a strange, cold knot tightened in my stomach. I didn't move to accept it. I just stared at the glowing invitation, my jaw slackening slightly as the reality of the situation settled over me, heavy and suffocating.

[story 2]

I almost dropped my phone. The sound of the notification chime seemed deafening in the quiet of my apartment. It was him. Liam. The boy who used to dismantle my lunch money with the casual cruelty of a surgeon. Now, a digital tether had been thrown across the years. I sat bolt upright on the sofa, my heart hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird. I slowly brought the device closer, my eyes scanning the profile, unable to process the sudden, bewildering shift in the landscape of my memory. A slow, involuntary exhale escaped my lips, and I blinked several times, trying to clear the sudden fog that had descended over my vision.

[story 3]

When the alert flashed, I froze mid-sip of my lukewarm coffee. The steam rising from the mug seemed to slow, suspended in the air. It was his username. Unmistakable. My hand, which had been reaching for the remote, simply stopped in mid-air. A strange, hollow feeling washed over my chest, a complete paralysis. I looked down at my hands, noticing the slight tremor in my grip. For a long moment, I couldn't form a coherent thought; my gaze remained fixed on the screen, absorbing the sheer absurdity of the invitation, my expression utterly blank, as if my brain had momentarily short-circuited.
